installation_steps.cmd: mkdir -p /opt/third_party && git clone https://github.com/ARISE-Initiative/robosuite.git /opt/third_party/robosuite && cd /opt/third_party/robosuite && git checkout v1.5.2 && uv pip install -e .

post_install_hooks.cmd: if ([ -f /workspace/robocasa/pyproject.toml ] || [ -f /workspace/robocasa/setup.py ]) && ! python -c 'import robocasa.macros_private' 2>/dev/null; then echo 'Setting up private macros...'; python /workspace/robocasa/scripts/setup_macros.py; fi

ROBOCASA_ASSETS="${ROBOCASA_ASSETS:-/workspace/robocasa/robocasa/models/assets}"; if [ ! -d "${ROBOCASA_ASSETS}/textures" ] || [ -z "$(ls -A "${ROBOCASA_ASSETS}/textures" 2>/dev/null)" ]; then echo 'Downloading kitchen assets (~5GB)...'; yes | python /workspace/robocasa/robocasa/scripts/download_kitchen_assets.py; else echo 'Kitchen assets already present, skipping.'; fi

readme_quotes: Clone robosuite (master), `pip install -e .` — from docs/introduction/installation.md step 3
Clone this repo ... `cd robocasa`, `pip install -e .` — from docs/introduction/installation.md step 4
uv.lock present at repo root with torch==2.4.0+cu118 — pyproject.toml [tool.uv.sources] torch = { index = 'pytorch-cu118' }
The latest version of RoboCasa uses the master branch of robosuite — docs/introduction/installation.md
This fork integrates policy-websocket so you can run RoboCasa with remote policies — README.md
ambiguities: README says 'master branch of robosuite' but the verified build pinned v1.5.2 (git tag). Keeping v1.5.2 for reproducibility — the prior run confirmed env built in 8.9s with this pin.

Ubuntu version: probe defaults to 22.04 but existing Dockerfile uses 20.04 (ubuntu20.04 is required because robosuite v1.5.2 was tested/verified on that base in the prior run). Keeping ubuntu20.04 via probe.json override.
needs_render_libs quirk: libegl1 and libosmesa6 are already in the explicit apt list — no separate injection needed.
policy-websocket dependency is declared in pyproject.toml and pulled by uv sync — no separate installation step needed.
